C:\Users\nilay\AppData\Roaming\npm\copilot.cmd --model gpt-5.4 --log-dir Y:\.sources\localm-tuts\courses\_examples\ctx-sdlc\lessons\08-operating-model\.output\logs --log-level debug --stream off --share Y:\.sources\localm-tuts\courses\_examples\ctx-sdlc\lessons\08-operating-model\.output\logs\session.md --add-dir Y:\.sources\localm-tuts\courses\_examples\ctx-sdlc\lessons\08-operating-model\src --allow-all-tools --allow-all-paths --deny-tool=powershell --deny-tool=sql --no-ask-user -p Inspect the lesson's context-maintenance artifacts before answering. Discover the relevant project instructions, audit scripts, clean and drifted examples, and maintenance docs that exist here rather than assuming a fixed file list. Then fix the drifted example at .github/examples/drifted/copilot-instructions.md by resolving all drift issues you find. Specifically: update stale technology references (Node.js version, logging library), remove contradictory rules (console.log vs structured logging), fix dead file path references (deleted helpers directory), remove over-specified inline code blocks that belong in scoped instructions, and align the drifted file with the clean example's conventions for accuracy and conciseness. Apply the fixes directly in the file. Do not run shell commands and do not use SQL.
